body_txt: The Beach Episode or Pool Episode is, simply put, an episode where the cast decided to take a break and go to the beach or a swimming pool for some wet and splashy fun. In a lot of shows, the Beach Episode generally exists solely for the purpose of getting the cast into bathing suits , either for Fanservice or just because it's yet another outfit to give to the characters. The main exception to this is in shows that are child-friendly, in which case this location is more likely to be used simply because the beach is typically considered fun... although you might get Parent Service anyway. Sometimes the bathing suits can be Played for Laughs if they look similar to the characters' already- Limited Wardrobes . For example, if Alice generally wears purple, expect her swimsuit to be purple, too. Occasionally, women may show up in a Sundress of Innocence instead. Of course, you can expect any combination of Beach Tropes to show up, but this is also a good spot for a random Summer Romance or Beauty Contest . Also, even if the cast isn't changing into bathing suits, they'll probably shed their footwear at the very least, which may set up someone to step on something sharp or jagged while barefoot (often followed by a Green Aesop if that something happens to be a piece of beach litter). If they don't go barefoot, they may be labeled a tourist like the derided " shoobie ". Beach episodes are also synonymous with Filler and Breather Episodes in arc-heavy narratives, being a more laidback story that allows the characters to de-stress from all the tension of the main plot. However, it's not uncommon for writers to subvert this audience expectation by ditching the laidback aspect to turn it into a more action-packed or dramatic one-off adventure (often through the appearance of a big storm or having a Threatening Shark or Sea Monster show up to attack) or foregoing the breather episode connotations of the setting to be plot-relevant by way of the main story interrupting things or having some vital character development occur. Common in, but hardly limited to, anime series. Often shows up in Side-Story Bonus Art . In anime and live-action television, a Beach Episode might overlap with the Vacation Episode , usually as a trip to a tropical locale. Expect one of the characters, usually female, to exclaim "It's the ocean!" when the gang first arrives on the scene. Cousin to the Pool Scene , the Hot Springs Episode (which usually takes place in highlands), and the Aquarium Date (which takes place in an aquarium). Also compare Palmtree Panic , which is when a level in a video game takes place on a beach.
